purpose: Cross-cutting asset-profile schema. Per-engine details live in <engine>.schema.json sibling files. Engine reads SCHEMA.json + <engine>.schema.json at startup.

sources.name: signInLogs
sources.description: Entra ID diagnostic — sign-in logs to LA

stages.name: bootstrap
stages.description: One-time setup (workspace IDs, DCR IDs, role grants)

stages.name: collect
stages.description: Raw fields, identity keys, freshness, lifecycle dates

stages.name: enrich
stages.description: Cross-source joins, time-window aggregates, transitive role expansion

stages.name: posture_analyze
stages.description: AssetType, AssetGroup, AI labels — AI lives only here

entityTypes.name: AadObjectId
entityTypes.normalization: EG emits 'tenantid=<guid>;objectid=<guid>'; canonicalize to bare <objectid> GUID; preserve original in Properties.collect.exposureGraph.EntityIds[*].id

entityTypes.name: SecurityIdentifier
entityTypes.carrier: AD object SID, Windows local SID

entityTypes.name: MdiSensorId
entityTypes.carrier: Defender for Identity sensor / unique entity ID

entityTypes.name: AzureVmId
entityTypes.carrier: Azure VM unique ID (distinct from AzureResourceId)

entityTypes.name: AwsResourceName
entityTypes.carrier: AWS ARN (multi-cloud via Defender for Cloud)

entityTypes.name: HostDeviceId
entityTypes.carrier: Parent device ID for WSL containers

identityTypes.name: User
identityTypes.description: Human or human-like account in Entra and/or AD

identityTypes.name: ServiceAccount
identityTypes.description: AD-side service account (gMSA, MSA, or regular SVC user). accountName ends in $; securityPrincipalType: MachineAccount

identityTypes.name: ServicePrincipal
identityTypes.description: Entra service principal (app registration, enterprise app, MS first-party)

identityTypes.name: ManagedIdentity
identityTypes.description: Azure system- or user-assigned managed identity

securityPrincipalTypes.name: MachineAccount
securityPrincipalTypes.description: AD computer or managed service account (objectClass: computer or gMSA). accountName ends with $.

uacFlagInterpretation.description: Same UAC flag means different things per identityType. See catalog UacFlag_Tiering.json for full matrix.

rules: No back-flow: stage X cannot read fields from a later stage
AI contained in posture_analyze; classify is deterministic and replayable
Stage Collect MUST NOT filter on classification fields (see collectionInclusivity)

skipFlags.effect: Stage Enrich no-ops; downstream sees null for stage:enrich fields
PostureAnalyze runs but skips AI call; only shortcuts + heuristic verdicts
Skip collect/enrich/posture; classify re-runs against most recent snapshot

collectionInclusivity.rule: Stage Collect MUST NOT filter assets on the presence or value of any classification field
collectionInclusivity.rationale: Born of a real bug: filtering SPN discovery on hasCriticality==1 dropped thousands of unclassified SPNs. Tier compute can't tier what was never collected.
allowed: Scope-limiting: where subscriptionId in (allowed-list), where tenantId == ourTenant

engineValidation.rule: dropped_due_to_predicate must be 0 for security predicates; non-zero raises an error

namingConventions.fieldNames: PascalCase. No SI_ prefix (table name carries it). No version numbers, no stage numbers.
namingConventions.sourceNamespaced: Prefix with source when clash possible: EntraRoles_Permanent, AzureRoles_HighestTier, Mde_LastSeen
namingConventions.booleans: Start with Is, Has, or Can: IsBreakGlass, HasLeakedCredentials

hashesSpec.truncation: 16 hex chars (64-bit, negligible collision risk per snapshot)

crossEngineDuplication.rule: When EG node has Categories: contains 'device' AND NodeLabel is an Azure resource type (e.g. microsoft.compute/virtualmachines), the asset emits TWO rows in TWO tables linked via shared EntityIds

entityIdsConflictResolution.decision: Pick one matching discovered subscription scope; surface rest in Properties.collect.exposureGraph.id_conflicts.AzureResourceId[]
Pick one matching SenseDeviceId; surface rest as historical/merged
Hard error — same EG node mapped to two Entra principals = data corruption. Log and skip.

supportedModes.description: Strictest tier across all contributors with weight > 0
Strictest tier among contributors with weight >= threshold
Recommended default — apply override:forced first, then weighted_min on survivors
Round-down weighted average — for AssetType when no source is authoritative

extensionModel.description: Schema is provider-agnostic. New providers (custom CL tables) add to vocabularies without engine code changes.
addNewSourceSteps: Optionally add aggregator contributors referencing the new fields
addNewDataSetSteps: Add data-set chapter to asset-profiling-schema.md (template: copy chapter 9/10/11 structure)
No engine-code change needed; validation runs at startup

ruleSet: stage.written_by exactly one value from vocabularies.stages
If source == derived then derivation block required
derivation.inputs[*].field must reference fields whose stage.written_by ≤ this field's stage in DAG
used_by[*].posture_rule must reference a real posture YAML rule by ID
Field with purpose:risk and used_by:[] → warn (dead hot column)
Posture rule references a field not in schema → fail-fast
Stage X writes a field whose stage.written_by != X → fail-fast
ai_context.include:true on a field with purpose:raw → fail-fast
Provider declared in engines.<e>.sources_consumed[] must exist in vocabularies.sources

openQuestions: EntityIds source attribution per entry vs parallel array? Spec proposes inline source for forensics; cost ~10% larger blob.
MdeDeviceId and AadDeviceId ever the same? If never, two separate EntityType entries; if sometimes, dedup rule needed.
Properties vs Raw column name? Properties matches existing v2.2 behavior.
Hash truncation 16 vs 32 hex chars? Spec proposes 16 (64-bit collision risk negligible per snapshot).
Should ai_shortcuts allow forced_tier_floor? Spec allows for high-confidence patterns (DC → T0).
